data.title: Option Care Health reports preliminary Q4, FY 2025 results, 2026 guidance mostly above estimate
data.description:
• Option Care Health (OPCH) expects Q4 2025 net revenue of $1.46B–$1.47B, compared with an estimate of $1.46B.
  • OPCH sees GAAP diluted EPS of $0.37–$0.39, vs estimate of $0.36.
  • Q4 2025 adjusted diluted EPS is projected at $0.46–$0.49, vs estimate of $0.46, with adjusted EBITDA of $123.7M–$127.7M.
  • Full-year 2025 net revenue is expected to be $5.65B–$5.66B, compared to an estimate of $5.64B.
  • OPCH expects GAAP diluted EPS of $1.27–$1.29.
  • Full-year 2025 adjusted diluted EPS is projected at $1.72–$1.76, adjusted EBITDA of $469M–$473M, and operating cash flow below $320M.
  • For full-year 2026, the company forecasts net revenue of $5.8B–$6B, compared to estimate of $6.06B.
  • 2026 adjusted diluted EPS expected between $1.82–$1.92, vs estimate of $1.49.
  • OPCH sees 2026 adjusted EBITDA of $480M–$505M.
  • OPCH shares up 3% post-market on Monday.

data.title: Okta in spotlight as Citi adds 90-day catalyst watch
data.description:
• Okta (OKTA) was in the spotlight on Monday as Citi added a 90-day catalyst watch to the cybersecurity firm.
  • “We expect OKTA to log a strong close to FY26, marked with bookings outperformance against receded idiosyncratic headwinds, that should support upward NTM revenue revisions and expectations,” analyst Fatima Boolani wrote in a note to clients. “This is on the back of favorable read-throughs for the Identity cyber sub-segment from our CISO survey (growing prioritization among CISOs; overwhelmingly preferred avenue for ‘securing agentic AI’), and healthy cRPO upside track record over the recent few Qs, all against FY27E consensus revenue growth we deem a low hurdle (~9% YoY). Anticipating strong F4Q26 results (~3pt cRPO growth beat to LDD levels) which should fuel upward revisions to FY27E topline outlook, we expect strengthened investor conviction on NTM+ fundamentals, which against a newly launched $1B buyback position the stock for a lift against current near-trough valuation of ~4x EV/S NTM and ~15x EV/FCF NTM.”
  • Boolani has a Neutral rating and $100 price target on Okta shares.

data.title: Medtronic partnering with Precision Neuroscience on brain-computer interface
data.description:
• Medtronic (MDT) will work with Precision Neuroscience to use the latter's Layer 7 brain-computer cortical interface with the former's StealthStation surgical navigation system.
  • Layer 7 is a thin film microelectrode array that captures electrical signals from the brain to translate brain activity.
  • Precision noted that currently, neurosurgeons often use separate devices to visualize brain structure and function, impacting the speed of intraoperative decision-making. The partnership will "deliver an end-to-end system designed to provide real-time functional and structural information in the operating room," according to a news release.

data.title: Option Care Health reports Preliminary Q4 results
data.description:
• Option Care Health press release (OPCH): Q4 Net revenue of $1.46 billion to $1.47 billion
  • GAAP net income of $59.1 million to $62.4 million
    • GAAP diluted EPS of $0.37 to $0.39
    • Adjusted diluted EPS of $0.46 to $0.49
    • Adjusted EBITDA of $123.7 million to $127.7 million

    Full Year 2025 Preliminary Expected Results

    • Net revenue of $5.645 billion to $5.655 billion
    • GAAP net income of $208.2 million to $211.5 million
    • GAAP diluted EPS of $1.27 to $1.29
    • Adjusted diluted EPS of $1.72 to $1.76
    • Adjusted EBITDA of $469.0 million to $473.0 million
    • Cash flow from operations below $320 million

    Full Year 2026 Preliminary Financial Guidance

    • Net revenue of $5.8 billion to $6.0 billion
    • Adjusted diluted EPS of $1.82 to $1.92
    • Adjusted EBITDA of $480 million to $505 million

data.title: Arqit Quantum files $250M mixed securities shelf offering
data.description:
• Arqit Quantum (ARQQ) filed a $250M mixed securities shelf offering on Monday.
  • Shares of the quantum computing firm were fractionally lower in extended trading after the announcement.

data.title: Citigroup to cut ~1,000 jobs this week - report
data.description:
Citigroup (C) plans to eliminate about 1,000 jobs this week as CEO and Chair Jane Fraser continues her campaign to streamline the bank and improve returns at the bank.

The reductions are part of the company's plan to cut 20,000 roles by the end of the year, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter. Citi (C) had 227K employees as of Sept. 30, 2025, down from 229K a year earlier.

Soon after Fraser took the CEO reins at the bank in 2021, she has focused on streamlining the company, pruning the number of markets the company operates in and condensing management layers to create a more efficient organization. 

“We will continue to reduce our headcount in 2026,” Citigroup (C) spokesperson said in a statement to Seeking Alpha. “These changes reflect adjustments we’re making to ensure our staffing levels, locations and expertise align with current business needs; efficiencies we have gained through technology; and progress against our transformation work.”

The bank is also set to issue its Q4 2025 results on Wednesday, Jan. 14. The company is expected to post Q4 non-GAAP EPS of $1.62, down from $2.26 in Q3 and up from $1.35 in Q4 2024.

Citi (C) stock ticked down 0.1% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Iron Mountain continues rally for seventh straight session
data.description:
Iron Mountain (IRM) extended its gains for the seventh consecutive session on Monday as its shares closed 0.35% higher at $89.50.

The company gained 7.15% in the preceding six sessions. On a year-to-date basis, the company’s shares have added nearly 7% compared to a 2% rise in the S&P500 index.

On Friday, the stock closed 2.14% higher at $89.19, adding to the stock’s rally.

As per Seeking Alpha’s quant rating, IRM has a Strong Sell rating with a score of 1.33 out of 5. The company has been rated D for momentum and a D+ for valuation and profitability. Seeking Alpha analysts also issued a Hold call for the stock. Wall Street analysts, however, are bullish on the stock and have rated it Buy.

Last month, Seeking Alpha analyst Dane Bowler rated the stock Buy on the back of a “compelling valuation after a sharp selloff.”

“While IRM does have an active data center pipeline, it is still a relatively small portion of its business. Thus, I thought it became overvalued as it got swept up in AI/data center excitement and is now coming back down with the AI bubble partially deflating toward the end of 2025,” the analyst said.

Another analyst, Juxtaposed Ideas, echoed a similar feeling and reiterated its Buy rating.

The analyst was bullish over the stock’s discounted rating and the company’s main monetization avenue through its legacy RIM/storage business.

“IRM is an even better Buy after the steep correction, thanks to the robust data center monetization trends and the resilient legacy RIM business for excellent capital appreciation/dividend returns,” they said.

data.title: Assertio regains Nasdaq compliance after bid price recovery
data.description:
• Assertio Holdings (ASRT) said on Monday that it has regained compliance with all applicable Nasdaq listing requirements, including the minimum bid price rule.
  • As of January 12, 2026, Nasdaq has determined that Assertio’s shares met the requirement to maintain a minimum bid price of $1.00 for at least 10 consecutive trading days, closing the matter.
  • The pharmaceutical company said the compliance followed the completion of a reverse stock split in December 2025.
  • ASRT closed +1.41% at $9.32.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: OneSpan acquires Build38
data.description:
• OneSpan (NASDAQ:OSPN) said on Monday it has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Build38.
  • Following the agreement, the company plans to integrate Build38’s SDK-based RASP technology, enhancing its mobile security portfolio, combined with Build38’s industry expertise and compliance with rigorous global certifications to deliver advanced mobile identity protection and security solutions.
  • The planned transaction is expected to close by March 2026.

data.title: Tech Voices: Nvidia-Lilly AI lab, Apple-Google, Meta's new president
data.description:
Seeking Alpha's roundup of statements, announcements, and remarks that could impact the technology sector. 

  • Nvidia (NVDA) and Eli Lilly (LLY) said they're investing up to $1B over five years to develop an AI-powered lab for drug discovery. 

The lab's infrastructure will be built on Nvidia's (NVDA) BioNeMo platform and Vera Rubin architecture. The companies also plan to utilize robotics and physical AI to speed and scale drug discovery and production. The facility will be located in the San Francisco Bay Area, they said. 

“AI is transforming every industry, and its most profound impact will be in life sciences,” said Nvidia CEO and founder Jensen Huang in a statement.

“Nvidia and Lilly are bringing together the best of our industries to invent a new blueprint for drug discovery—one where scientists can explore vast biological and chemical spaces in silico before a single molecule is made," Huang added.

“Combining our volumes of data and scientific knowledge with Nvidia’s computational power and model-building expertise could reinvent drug discovery as we know it," Lilly Chair and CEO David Ricks said in the statement.

"By bringing together world-class talent in a startup environment, we’re creating the conditions for breakthroughs that neither company could achieve alone," Ricks added.

  • Apple (AAPL) said its future Apple Intelligence features, including an updated version of Siri, will be based on Google's (GOOG) (GOOGL) Gemini under a new multi-year collaboration deal. 

"After careful evaluation, Apple determined that Google's Al technology provides the most capable foundation for Apple Foundation Models and is excited about the innovative new experiences it will unlock for Apple users," Apple said in a blog post.

"Apple Intelligence will continue to run on Apple devices and Private Cloud Compute, while maintaining Apple's industry-leading privacy standards," Apple added.

  • Meta Platforms (META) has hired former Trump advisor Dina Powell McCormick as its president and vice chairman. 

“Dina’s experience at the highest levels of global finance, combined with her deep relationships around the world, makes her uniquely suited to help Meta manage this next phase of growth as the company’s President and Vice Chairman,” said Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg in a statement.

McCormick served as a Meta board member last year but resigned in December. A former partner at Goldman Sachs, she served as deputy national security advisor for the first Trump administration.
